hypothesis: Detector discrimination is more sensitive than rubric-based judging for evaluating top-tier models
rationale: Detector ran cleanly and produced differentiated, non-trivial scores. Both personas score well below the 0.5 realism_proxy threshold — classifier accuracy is 0.0, meaning neither persona was flagged as obviously AI-generated. This is a strong positive signal: the personas evade the detector, establishing a high-quality baseline. The sole AI tell detected (overly_balanced — goals and pains arrays have matching lengths) is a minor structural artifact, not a content deficiency. The detector is now a viable realism metric: future experiments can track whether prompt changes push detectability scores up (bad) or down (good). Hypothesis partially validated — detector gives a more granular 0-1 score vs. pass/fail rubric judging.
